items.title: Euro-Area Outlook Cut By Economists as Iran War Weighs on Region
items.description: Growth in the euro zone is set to be weaker this year than previously predicted after hostilities in the Middle East resumed, economists say.

items.title: Healthcare Looks Ready to Star in Rotation Trade
items.description: A compelling argument is growing for healthcare stocks to be among the big winners in the rotation to more defensive parts of the market as the sector’s outlook improves.

items.title: Bridgepoint Buys Large Stake in Danish Drone Firm UMag Solutions
items.description: UK-listed private capital firm Bridgepoint Group is acquiring a majority stake in UMag Solutions ApS, a Danish defense technology company, adding to a raft of deals involving dronemakers.

items.title: FTSE 100 Futures, Pound Fall as Iran Tensions Ramp Up
TSMC Sales Surge 36% in Fresh Sign of AI Spending Momentum
items.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reported quarterly sales rose 36%, meeting high expectations and signaling global demand for AI hardware remains intact.

items.title: US and Iran Trade Fresh Strikes, Dispute Whether Hormuz Is Open
items.description: The US and Iran exchanged fresh strikes overnight into Monday as they continued their tit-for-tat attacks while issuing conflicting declarations over whether the Strait of Hormuz was open to shipping.

items.title: South Africa Plans First Strategic Oil Boost Since Apartheid Era
items.description: South Africa plans to increase its strategic oil reserves for the first time since the apartheid government stockpiled crude, adding to measures across the continent to mitigate supply shocks.

items.title: Base Metals Decline as US, Iran Flare-Up Clouds Demand Outlook
items.description: Base metals retreated after renewed hostilities between the US and Iran fueled concerns over global economic growth, clouding the outlook for industrial commodities.

items.description: US consumers are already on edge after prices never fell from post-Covid inflation.

items.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi used a three-nation tour to deepen India’s Indo-Pacific partnerships, Bloomberg's Swati Pandey reports. (Source: Bloomberg)
